advantages: Persistent record — investigations work hours/days after the fact.
Hierarchical loggers + cascading config let you tune one subsystem at a time.
Structured (JSON) logs unlock log-store querying, dashboards and alerting.
Production-safe — unlike debuggers, logs don't pause your program.
disadvantages: Verbose logs hide signal in noise; under-logged systems hide everything else.
Cost: log volume = storage + ingest fees; spikes during incidents.
Sensitive data leaks (PII, secrets) into logs are real incidents waiting to happen.
Mis-configured library logging fights the application's config — confusing failures.
Logs without correlation IDs are nearly useless in concurrent systems.
